elements.sent-2411-001: In this paper we generalize the concept of classical spectral triples by extending the framework from Hilbert spaces to $L^p$-spaces, and from C*-algebras to $L^p$-operator algebras, $p \in [1, \infty)$.
elements.sent-2411-002: Specifically, we construct $L^p$-spectral triples for reduced group $L^p$-operator algebras and for $L^p$ UHF-algebras of infinite tensor product type.
elements.sent-2411-003: Furthermore, inspired by Christensen and Ivan’s construction of a Dirac operator on AF C*-algebras, we provide an $L^p$ version of this construction for $L^p$ UHF-algebras.

elements.sent-2411-005: Over the last decade, the study of operator algebras on $L^p$-spaces has gained increasing interest.
elements.sent-2411-006: This trend has been marked by the extension to their $L^p$-analogues of various constructions traditionally associated with $C^*$-algebras.
elements.sent-2411-007: Notably, algebras such as Cuntz algebras \cite{ncp2012AC , group and groupoid algebras \cite{NCP13, GL17 , crossed products \cite{NCP13 , and those arising from finite-dimensional constructions such as AF and UHF-algebras \cite{NCP13_2, ncpmgv2017AF , have all found generalizations in the context of $L^p$-spaces.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-008: Spectral triples, whose origin stems from an axiomatic characterization of the notion of unbounded selfadjoint operators with compact resolvent on Hilbert spaces, represent a significant construction in the realm of noncommutative geometry.
elements.sent-2411-009: Their study was initiated by Alain Connes in the late 1980's \cite{Connes89 .}
elements.sent-2411-010: These triples provide a framework for extracting geometric data from operator algebras, particularly through the quantum metric they induce on the state space of their C*-algebras via constructions pioneered by Connes and Rieffel \cite{Connes89, R98, R99, Rieffel00 ; see also \cite{AguLat, Rieffel17,FLP24, Latremoliere16b, LoWu21 .}}
elements.sent-2411-011: When the weak-$*$ topology on the state space of a C*-algebra agrees with the topology induced by such a quantum metric, the state space is called a quantum compact metric space.
elements.sent-2411-012: These spaces, thought of as a noncommutative analogue of the algebra of Lipschitz functions on a compact metric space, can be obtained in other ways that do not require the use of spectral triples (\cite{AguLat, Latremoliere16b ), and have been thoroughly studied in the past two decades.}
elements.sent-2411-013: While the work by Connes focuses on spectral triple aspects, Rieffel starts by considering metrics on ``state-like spaces'' of Banach algebras.
elements.sent-2411-014: Indeed, Rieffel's original definitions in \cite{R98 were already cast in a general Banach algebras context and did not require a C*-algebra structure.}
elements.sent-2411-015: This makes Rieffel's work a natural place to start when looking for analogues outside of the C*-algebra setting.
elements.sent-2411-016: In this paper, we propose a generalization of the concept of spectral triples by replacing the Hilbert space framework with a more general $L^p$-space setting.
elements.sent-2411-017: We demonstrate that Connes' classical spectral triple example for group algebras extends to this $L^p$-based framework.
elements.sent-2411-018: Additionally, building on the work of Christensen and Ivan \cite{CI , who constructed a Dirac operator for AF-algebras, we present an $L^p$-analogue for UHF-algebras of infinite tensor product type, as defined by Phillips in \cite{NCP13_2 .}}
